proof_assertions: EXP-1a: isDualAuthExpired returns true at exact boundary
EXP-1b: isApprovalExpired returns true at exact boundary
EXP-1c: both contracts agree — boundary is expired
EXP-2: isDualAuthExpired — exact boundary is expired
EXP-2b: isApprovalExpired — exact boundary is expired
EXP-3a: isDualAuthExpired — 1ms before boundary is valid
EXP-3b: isApprovalExpired — 1ms before boundary is valid
EXP-4a: isDualAuthExpired — 1ms after boundary is expired
EXP-4b: isApprovalExpired — 1ms after boundary is expired
EXP-5: replayApprovalChain — replay reproduces same expiry rejection
EXP-5b: replay expiration chain — deterministic from (issuedAt, kind)
EXP-6a: completeApproval — throws at exact boundary
EXP-6c: assertApprovalNotExpired — throws at exact boundary
change_summary: Normalized dual-auth.ts isDualAuthExpired() from > to >= boundary semantics, matching approval-expiration.ts canonical policy. completeApproval() now calls isDualAuthExpired() directly (DRY). dual-auth-matrix.json documents the boundary invariant.
